Everything looks great. Here's a summary of everything created for the `@ai-sdk/pixelart` provider:

---

## ✅ `packages/pixelart/` — PixelArt Provider

### Files created

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `package.json` | Package manifest — mirrors `@ai-sdk/google` structure, `workspace:*` deps on `@ai-sdk/provider` and `@ai-sdk/provider-utils` |
| `tsconfig.json` | TypeScript config extending `@vercel/ai-tsconfig/ts-library.json` with project references to `../provider` and `../provider-utils` |
| `tsconfig.build.json` | Build-time tsconfig that excludes test files |
| `src/pixelart-image-settings.ts` | `PixelArtImageModelId` type + `PixelArtImageSettings` interface with `size`, `style`, `pixelDensity` |
| `src/pixelart-image-model.ts` | `PixelArtImageModel` class implementing `ImageModelV3` |
| `src/pixelart-provider.ts` | `createPixelArt()` factory + `PixelArtProvider` interface + `pixelart` singleton |
| `src/index.ts` | Public barrel export |

### Key design decisions

- **`PixelArtProvider`** does **not** extend `ProviderV3` (which requires `languageModel`/`embeddingModel`) since this is an image-only provider — it just has `specificationVersion: 'v3'`, `image()`, and `imageModel()`.
- **`size`, `style`, `pixelDensity`** can be set at the model level via `PixelArtImageSettings` (passed to `.image(modelId, settings)`) or overridden per-call via `providerOptions.pixelart`.
- **`aspectRatio`** emits an `unsupported` warning (the PixelArt API uses `size` instead).
- **Image editing** (`files`/`mask`) throws clearly, as PixelArt is generation-only.
- **Base URL** defaults to `https://api.pixelart.ai/v1`; auth via `Authorization: Bearer <PIXELART_API_KEY>`.
- Follows the same Zod schema patterns (`lazySchema` + `zodSchema` + `InferSchema`) as `@ai-sdk/google`.
